page: Amazon Web Services x8aedz.large is a memory-optimized instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features an AMD EPYC 9R05 processor with 2 dedicated physical cores operating at a high clock speed of 5.0 GHz. The system is equipped with 64.0 GB of DDR5 memory running at 6 GHz, yielding an exceptionally high memory-to-core ratio of 32.0 GB per core. Additionally, it includes a bundled 158 GB local NVMe SSD instance store volume and provides a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps.

Benchmark results highlight outstanding single-core performance. The instance achieves top-tier status in stress-ng single-core CPU tests, single-threaded gzip compression, and Geekbench single-core evaluations. However, multi-core performance metrics, such as stress-ng multi-core and Geekbench multi-core scores, fall into the average tier due to the 2-core configuration. Memory bandwidth and database operations, including Redis SET performance, also register in the average range, while certain graphics-adjacent tasks like Geekbench ray tracing are weak.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the x8aedz.large offers a specialized resource density, combining high-frequency single-core execution with massive memory capacity and fast local NVMe storage. This profile makes it highly suitable for memory-intensive workloads that do not require massive parallelization, such as small-to-medium database hosting, single-threaded data processing pipelines, caching layers, and text processing applications.
description: Amazon Web Services x8aedz.large is a memory-optimized instance featuring an AMD EPYC 9R05 processor with 2 dedicated physical cores running at 5.0 GHz. It is equipped with 64.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory, providing an exceptional 32.0 GB of RAM per core. The instance includes a bundled 158 GB local NVMe SSD for high-speed temporary storage and delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps. Benchmarks indicate top-tier single-core CPU performance and single-threaded compression speeds, though multi-core metrics remain average due to the dual-core design. This hardware profile offers high resource density for memory-heavy, single-thread-bound tasks. It is well-suited for database hosting, caching layers, and text processing workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services x8aedz.large is a memory-optimized instance featuring 2 AMD EPYC vCPUs at 5.0 GHz, 64 GB of DDR5 RAM, and a 158 GB local NVMe SSD, delivering top-tier single-core performance.

tagline: High-frequency dual-core processing paired with massive DDR5 memory and local NVMe storage for memory-intensive single-threaded workloads.
